definition:
κινέω, -ῶ 
 [in LXX for נוּעַ hi., רָמַשׂ, etc. ;] 
  1. to set in motion, move: Mat.23:4; τ. κεφαλήν (Psa.22:8, al.), Mat.27:39, Mrk.15:29; pass. (cf. Gen.7:21), Act.17:28. 
   2. to remove: Rev.2:5 6:14. 
   3. to excite, stir up: Act.24:5; pass., Act.21:30 (cf. μετα-, συν-κινέω).†
 (AS)
